summary: Project to build an app focused on Skin Care Products.
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Minimalist skincare app logo, line-art style, warm neutral palette (skincare-logo.dim_512x512.png)
- Clean hero illustration featuring abstract skincare bottles and botanical elements, soft warm neutrals, lots of whitespace (skincare-hero.dim_1600x600.png)

existing_features.title: Build a React UI for browsing skin care products: a home/catalog page that shows a product grid/list with product image, name, brand, category, skinType, and price, plus a search input and filter controls (category, skin type, concern, price range).
Add a product details view that opens from the catalog (route or modal) and displays all product fields, including description, key ingredients, concerns, and usage instructions.
Add an authenticated management UI for products: when signed in, provide screens/forms to create a new product and edit/delete existing products, including client-side validation for required fields.
Use React Query for product fetching/mutations with proper cache invalidation after create/update/delete so the catalog and details stay in sync without manual refresh.

Establish a coherent visual theme for the app (colors, typography, spacing, components) suited to a skin care catalog—clean, minimalist, warm neutral palette—and apply it consistently across catalog, filters, details, and forms (Tailwind + existing UI components).
Add generated static image assets to the frontend and use them in the UI (stored under frontend/public/assets/generated): app logo and a homepage hero/banner illustration.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create a skin care product data model and persistent storage in the Motoko backend, including fields: id, name, brand, category (e.g., cleanser/serum/moisturizer/sunscreen), skinType (e.g., oily/dry/combination/sensitive), keyIngredients (list of strings), concerns (list of strings), price (number + currency string), description, usageInstructions, and imageUrl (string).

Implement backend canister methods to manage products: listProducts (with optional query parameters for search text, category, skinType, concerns, minPrice, maxPrice), getProductById, createProduct, updateProduct, and deleteProduct.
Add simple authorization rules: only authenticated users (via Internet Identity principal) can create, update, or delete products; anonymous users can only browse.
